definition_en: To send out a programme by radio or television
example: The ceremony was broadcast live to millions of viewers.

definition_en: A film or TV programme presenting real events
example: The documentary on climate change won an Oscar.

definition_en: Watching or listening to content over the internet in real time
example: Streaming services have replaced traditional TV for many people.

definition_en: The measure of how many people are watching a programme
example: The show achieved record ratings on its first night.

definition_en: A journalist who reports from a particular place or on a particular subject
example: The foreign correspondent reported from the conflict zone.

definition_en: One part of a TV or radio series
example: I watched three episodes in a row last night.

definition_en: The title of a news article printed in large letters
example: The story made the front-page headline.

definition_en: A tendency to favour one side when reporting news
example: Critics accused the channel of political bias.

definition_en: The suppression of information or speech by authorities
example: Press censorship limits freedom of expression.

definition_en: A digital audio programme available to listen to online
example: She hosts a weekly podcast about personal finance.

definition_en: The number of copies of a newspaper sold on a regular basis
example: Print newspaper circulation has fallen sharply.

definition_en: The main presenter of a news programme
example: The anchor introduced each story with a brief summary.

definition_en: A new series or product that comes from an already existing one
example: The spin-off series became more popular than the original.

definition_en: False or inaccurate information that is spread unintentionally
example: Misinformation about health can be dangerous.

definition_en: The use of humour or irony to criticise politics or society
example: The TV show is a satire of modern politics.

definition_en: A film or book that continues the story of a previous one
example: The sequel was even more successful than the original.

definition_en: An evaluation of a film, book or show published for the public
example: The film received glowing reviews from critics.

definition_en: The hours of the evening when the most people are watching TV
example: The final was broadcast in prime time.

definition_en: A person who pays to receive a regular publication or service
example: The channel reached one million subscribers.

definition_en: A conversation where a journalist asks questions to a public figure
example: The president gave an exclusive interview to the newspaper.
